name: nih-chestxray14

env_axis.name: hospital
env_axis.shift_mechanism: single-site here; cross-country/cross-hospital realized when paired with CheXpert (Stanford) and PadChest (Alicante ES)

ood_protocol.name: cxr-cross-country
ood_protocol.ref: Cohen et al. 2020; Pooch et al. 2020; Wang et al. 2017 CVPR (dataset paper)

facts: 112,120 frontal CXR / 30,805 patients (NIH Bethesda); 14 thorax labels NLP-extracted from radiology reports.
hypotheses: Label-pipeline shift (NIH NLP vs CheXpert NLP vs PadChest manual) dominates over population shift, and the three-way pairing can separate the two.
Foundation-model performance on NIH exposes a label-signature distinct from the image-signature.
intuitions: Multi-cohort foundation-model evaluations from 2024-25 (CXR-Foundation, MedImageInsight, BioViL) likely report NIH-specific failure modes.
Comparisons that add MIMIC-CXR as a fourth leg probably exist in the literature, though it is excluded from this corpus.
unknowns: Radiologist-disagreement noise on uncertain multi-label cases cannot be separated from genuine label noise in the data, and there is no established label distinguishing 'disagreement-as-data' from 'noise-as-data'.
epistemic_state.boundary: T=1 (study order weakly present); cannot drive temporal-shift claims. Process P=0 — no causal chain in the image itself.

representational_commitments: Labels via NLP from reports — label noise tied to NLP pipeline choice
Study-as-unit rather than patient-trajectory — temporal dependencies within patient ignored
14-class taxonomy is the NIH curators' choice
materialization_status: raw uploaded recursive to gs://traces-corpus/snapshots/nih-chestxray14/v1-2017-hf-mirror/ (43 GB). Source: HuggingFace mirror (alkzar90/NIH-Chest-X-ray-dataset) which contains the official NIH release. Per-file SHAs deferred; manifest-level integrity via the HuggingFace mirror's content-addressed storage.
